summary: Acoustic gems, seamless China>Rider, soaring Dark Star
blurb: The show opens with an intimate acoustic set highlighted by Little Sadie and a radiant "I've Been All Around This World" before launching into a fiery electric half. The China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue is flawless, leading to a transcendent Dark Star. Jerry’s Strat shines, Phil’s bass is on point, and Tom Constanten’s organ drives the second‑set energy.
review: On December 26 1969 at McFarlin Auditorium in Dallas, the Dead delivered one of their most captivating early‑year performances. An understated acoustic first set – featuring Little Sadie, Long Black Limousine, and a luminous rendition of "I've Been All Around This World" – gave way to an electric second half that built momentum with a perfect China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition, a tight High Time, and a soaring Dark Star that showcased Jerry Garcia’s inventive Fender Strat voice. Phil Lesh’s bass was described as especially strong, his Alembic‑modified Gibson tone anchoring the band while Tom Constanten’s B‑3 organ added drive to the jam‑filled latter set. The show rounds out with a solid New Speedway Boogie (the lone weak spot) and a rousing Turn On Your Lovelight, making it a must‑listen snapshot of the band's evolving sound.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described with strong positive language – "crystal‑clear", "breathtaking acoustic set", "soaring Dark Star" and "mighty tasty electric set" – and highlight the same standout songs (e.g., Dark Star, China>Rider). No negative comments appear across analyses, indicating consistent praise for performance.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture that faithfully renders the acoustic set and the soaring Dark Star with excellent detail and no noticeable drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Breathtaking acoustic set featuring Little Sadie and "I've Been All Around This World"
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue that builds momentum
Transcendent Dark Star with inventive guitar work and strong organ drive
Phil Lesh’s bass tone shines throughout, anchoring the band
song_highlights: I've Been All Around This World

band_performance.Jerry: Bright Fender Strat tone, inventive ideas on Dark Star and overall stellar guitar work.
band_performance.Phil: Especially strong bass performance with a notable Alembic‑modified Gibson EB‑0 tone, providing a solid foundation.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal harmonies are spot‑on throughout the show.
band_performance.Brent: Tom Constanten’s B‑3 organ is inspired and drives the energy of the second set.

must_listen_sequences: I've Been All Around This World
